# Architecture Patterns With Python (Harry Percival & Bob Gregory)

Before writing, modifying, or planning any code, you MUST use your `read_file` tool to load the relevant guidelines from `.roo/vendor/`. You may read multiple files if the task crosses domains.

## Table of Contents:
    - [Domain Modeling](.roo/vendor/python/architecture-patterns-with-python-harry-percival-bob-gregory/01-domain-modeling.md): The specific problem you are trying to solve or the set of activities that business processes support.
    - [Repository Pattern](.roo/vendor/python/architecture-patterns-with-python-harry-percival-bob-gregory/02-repository-pattern.md): Python backend development tasks involving database access, Object-Relational Mapping (ORM) setup (particularly SQLAlchemy), unit and integration testing, and architectural refactoring aimed at implementing Domain-Driven Design (DDD), Hexagonal Architecture, Onion Architecture, or Ports and Adapters.
    - [A Brief Interlude: On Coupling and Abstractions](.roo/vendor/python/architecture-patterns-with-python-harry-percival-bob-gregory/03-a-brief-interlude-on-coupling-and-abstractions.md): Refactoring existing Python code, designing new system architectures, writing unit or integration tests, handling I/O operations (filesystem, network, databases), or addressing issues related to tight coupling and untestable code.
    - [Our First Use Case: Flask API and Service Layer](.roo/vendor/python/architecture-patterns-with-python-harry-percival-bob-gregory/04-our-first-use-case-flask-api-and-service-layer.md): Triggered when the user requests the creation, refactoring, or testing of API endpoints (e.g., Flask), Service Layer orchestrations, or application folder structures in Python projects utilizing Domain-Driven Design (DDD) and layered architectures.
    - [TDD in High Gear and Low Gear](.roo/vendor/python/architecture-patterns-with-python-harry-percival-bob-gregory/05-tdd-in-high-gear-and-low-gear.md): Trigger these rules when the user requests test creation, test refactoring, implementation of Test-Driven Development (TDD), service layer modifications, or end-to-end (E2E) / integration test setup. Activate specifically when resolving test coupling issues, managing test data setup, or defining service layer APIs.
    - [Unit of Work Pattern](.roo/vendor/python/architecture-patterns-with-python-harry-percival-bob-gregory/06-unit-of-work-pattern.md): The domain encompasses tasks related to database transactions, atomic operations, data integrity, persistent storage abstraction, integration testing for database states, and refactoring Service Layers, API endpoints, or Repositories to remove direct ORM/database session dependencies.
    - [Aggregates and Consistency Boundaries](.roo/vendor/python/architecture-patterns-with-python-harry-percival-bob-gregory/07-aggregates-and-consistency-boundaries.md): Triggered when the user request involves designing or modifying domain models, establishing consistency boundaries, building Repositories, structuring object graphs, implementing database concurrency controls (locking/versioning), or writing tests for data integrity and concurrent transactions.
    - [Events and the Message Bus](.roo/vendor/python/architecture-patterns-with-python-harry-percival-bob-gregory/08-events-and-the-message-bus.md): Python applications using Domain-Driven Design (DDD), the Repository pattern, the Unit of Work (UoW) pattern, or the Service Layer pattern, specifically involving side-effects, secondary workflows, notifications, cross-aggregate communications, Event-Driven Architecture, or Message Bus implementations.
    - [Going to Town on the Message Bus](.roo/vendor/python/architecture-patterns-with-python-harry-percival-bob-gregory/09-going-to-town-on-the-message-bus.md): The domain encompasses tasks involving the Service Layer, API endpoints, the Message Bus, domain events, workflow orchestration, and the refactoring of existing service functions into an event-driven architecture.
    - [Commands and Command Handler](.roo/vendor/python/architecture-patterns-with-python-harry-percival-bob-gregory/10-commands-and-command-handler.md): message buses, event-driven architecture, command handlers, message dispatching, or error recovery in distributed systems/messaging loops
    - [Event-Driven Architecture: Using Events to Integrate Microservices](.roo/vendor/python/architecture-patterns-with-python-harry-percival-bob-gregory/11-event-driven-architecture-using-events-to-integrate-microservices.md): Integrating microservices, designing inter-service communication, implementing asynchronous messaging architectures, defining message brokers (e.g., Redis Pub/Sub, Kafka, RabbitMQ), writing event consumers/publishers, or refactoring synchronous HTTP/RPC calls between services into event-driven workflows.
    - [Command-Query Responsibility Segregation (CQRS)](.roo/vendor/python/architecture-patterns-with-python-harry-percival-bob-gregory/12-command-query-responsibility-segregation-cqrs.md): Designing, implementing, or refactoring data access patterns, APIs, or architectural structures involving read and write operations, specifically Command-Query Responsibility Segregation (CQRS), Command-Query Separation (CQS), database views, read/write models, event handlers updating state, or performance optimizations for data retrieval.
    - [Dependency Injection (and Bootstrapping)](.roo/vendor/python/architecture-patterns-with-python-harry-percival-bob-gregory/13-dependency-injection-and-bootstrapping.md): This rule set activates when the AI is tasked with adding, modifying, or refactoring application dependencies, creating adapters for external systems, configuring application startup/initialization, implementing message buses, writing service-layer handlers, or writing unit/integration tests that interact with external state (I/O, database, notifications) in a Python-based Event-Driven Architecture.
